name: pyyaml

metadata.name: PyYAML
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: YAML parser and emitter for Python

documentation:
YAML is a data serialization format designed for human readability
and interaction with scripting languages.  PyYAML is a YAML parser
and emitter for Python.

PyYAML features a complete YAML 1.1 parser, Unicode support, pickle
support, capable extension API, and sensible error messages.  PyYAML
supports standard YAML tags and provides Python-specific tags that
allow to represent an arbitrary Python object.

PyYAML is applicable for a broad range of tasks from complex
configuration files to object serialization and persistence.

modules.name: lib._yaml
documentation: This is a stub package designed to roughly emulate the _yaml
extension module, which previously existed as a standalone module
and has been moved into the `yaml` package namespace.
It does not perfectly mimic its old counterpart, but should get
close enough for anyone who's relying on it even when they shouldn't.

modules.name: examples.pygments-lexer.yaml
documentation:
yaml.py

Lexer for YAML, a human-friendly data serialization language
(http://yaml.org/).

Written by Kirill Simonov <xi@resolvent.net>.

License: Whatever suitable for inclusion into the Pygments package.

Parse the first YAML document in a stream

Parse all YAML documents in a stream

Resolve only basic YAML tags. This is known

Parse all YAML documents in a stream

functions.name: lib.yaml.emit
documentation:
Emit YAML parsing events into a stream.
If stream is None, return the produced string instead.

functions.name: lib.yaml.serialize_all
documentation:
Serialize a sequence of representation trees into a YAML stream.
If stream is None, return the produced string instead.

functions.name: lib.yaml.serialize
documentation:
Serialize a representation tree into a YAML stream.
If stream is None, return the produced string instead.

functions.name: lib.yaml.dump_all
documentation:
Serialize a sequence of Python objects into a YAML stream.
If stream is None, return the produced string instead.

functions.name: lib.yaml.dump
documentation:
Serialize a Python object into a YAML stream.
If stream is None, return the produced string instead.

functions.name: lib.yaml.safe_dump_all
documentation:
Serialize a sequence of Python objects into a YAML stream.
Produce only basic YAML tags.
If stream is None, return the produced string instead.

functions.name: lib.yaml.safe_dump
documentation:
Serialize a Python object into a YAML stream.
Produce only basic YAML tags.
If stream is None, return the produced string instead.

functions.name: lib.yaml.add_implicit_resolver
documentation:
Add an implicit scalar detector.
If an implicit scalar value matches the given regexp,
the corresponding tag is assigned to the scalar.
first is a sequence of possible initial characters or None.

functions.name: lib.yaml.add_path_resolver
documentation:
Add a path based resolver for the given tag.
A path is a list of keys that forms a path
to a node in the representation tree.
Keys can be string values, integers, or None.

functions.name: lib.yaml.add_constructor
documentation:
Add a constructor for the given tag.
Constructor is a function that accepts a Loader instance
and a node object and produces the corresponding Python object.

functions.name: lib.yaml.add_multi_constructor
documentation:
Add a multi-constructor for the given tag prefix.
Multi-constructor is called for a node if its tag starts with tag_prefix.
Multi-constructor accepts a Loader instance, a tag suffix,
and a node object and produces the corresponding Python object.

functions.name: lib.yaml.add_representer
documentation:
Add a representer for the given type.
Representer is a function accepting a Dumper instance
and an instance of the given data type
and producing the corresponding representation node.

functions.name: lib.yaml.add_multi_representer
documentation:
Add a representer for the given type.
Multi-representer is a function accepting a Dumper instance
and an instance of the given data type or subtype
and producing the corresponding representation node.

classes.name: lib.yaml.loader.UnsafeLoader
documentation: UnsafeLoader is the same as Loader (which is and was always unsafe on
untrusted input). Use of either Loader or UnsafeLoader should be rare, since
FullLoad should be able to load almost all YAML safely. Loader is left intact

modules.name: lib.yaml.reader
documentation: This module contains abstractions for the input stream. You don't have to
looks further, there are no pretty code.
It's just a record and its only use is producing nice error messages.
Parser does not use it for any other purposes.
Reader determines the encoding of `data` and converts it to unicode.
Reader provides the following methods and attributes:
reader.forward(length=1) - move the current position to `length` characters.
reader.index - the number of the current character.
reader.line, stream.column - the line and the column of the current character.

methods.name: lib.yaml.reader.Reader.__init__
documentation: - determines the data encoding and converts it to a unicode string,
- checks if characters are in allowed range,
- a file-like object with its `read` method returning `str`,
- a file-like object with its `read` method returning `unicode`.

modules.name: lib.yaml.parser
documentation: The following YAML grammar is LL(1) and is parsed by a recursive descent
block_node: { ALIAS TAG ANCHOR SCALAR BLOCK-SEQUENCE-START BLOCK-MAPPING-START FLOW-SEQUENCE-START FLOW-MAPPING-START }
flow_node: { ALIAS ANCHOR TAG SCALAR FLOW-SEQUENCE-START FLOW-MAPPING-START }
block_content: { BLOCK-SEQUENCE-START BLOCK-MAPPING-START FLOW-SEQUENCE-START FLOW-MAPPING-START SCALAR }
block_node_or_indentless_sequence: { ALIAS ANCHOR TAG SCALAR BLOCK-SEQUENCE-START BLOCK-MAPPING-START FLOW-SEQUENCE-START FLOW-MAPPING-START BLOCK-ENTRY }
flow_sequence_entry: { ALIAS ANCHOR TAG SCALAR FLOW-SEQUENCE-START FLOW-MAPPING-START KEY }
flow_mapping_entry: { ALIAS ANCHOR TAG SCALAR FLOW-SEQUENCE-START FLOW-MAPPING-START KEY }

methods.name: lib.yaml.parser.Parser.parse_flow_sequence_first_entry
documentation: Note that while production rules for both flow_sequence_entry and
flow_mapping_entry are equal, their interpretations are different.
